IT HISTORY
Shockley Brought Silicon, Hennessy Scaled the Model — Google Was Just the Latest Link in a 70-year Chain Reaction

7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

creator genenrate by arkSilicon Valley has a brain, and it sits on an old horse farm in Palo Alto. For more than a century, the people, patents, and companies that run the tech industry — Google, Cisco, Sun, Yahoo, LinkedIn, Instagram, and tens of thousands more — have come out of Stanford University. This is the story of how that brain got built, in five parts.

Silicon Valley Brain

William Shockley. Chuck Painter / Stanford News Service, CC BY 3.0, via Wikimedia Commons.In 1955, William Shockley picked up the telephone and called Frederick Terman.
Shockley had just co-invented the transistor at Bell Labs, work that would win him a Nobel Prize, but he had no interest in staying in New Jersey to publish papers. He wanted to start a…
